key_insight: Raising cold-spray pressure from 30 to 60 bar increases copper particle velocity past the bonding threshold, producing denser deposits with better conductivity and ductility through stronger inter-particle bonding and recrystallization-related refinement.
engineering_relevance: For copper CSAM, spray pressure is a key control knob for tuning the trade-off between mechanical integrity and electrical performance in additively manufactured parts.
method: LightSPEE3D cold spray copper deposits were produced at 30, 40, 50, and 60 bar.
Microstructure and crystallite evolution were assessed with SEM and XRD.
Hardness, eddy current conductivity, and tensile testing quantified property changes.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports pressure-dependent improvements in particle velocity, conductivity, and ductility, with XRD indicating crystallite refinement and higher dislocation density at increased pressure.
watch_item: Higher pressure reduced hardness via recovery/recrystallization effects, so the optimum window must balance strength, ductility, and conductivity rather than maximize only one property.
paper_title: Optimising Cold Spray Additive Manufacturing: Pressure-driven enhancement of mechanical performance in copper deposits
